sourcesSearched: https://academicpositions.be | .nl | .com (KU Leuven, Ghent, Hasselt, VUB mirrors)
discoveryNotes: Wave-2 refresh (as-of 2026-07-12) re-verifies both prior discovery runs for this region: the original agent-batch-3 sweep (Netherlands + Luxembourg, 9 offers) and the standalone Belgium wave-2 deep scan (batch-wave2-belgium.json, 9 offers). All 18 previously-verified open offers were re-checked against their live source pages (AcademicTransfer/Maastricht/TU Delft vacancy portals, KU Leuven jobsite mirrors, jobs.ugent.be, jobs.vub.be, EURAXESS, University of Luxembourg HR portal) on 2026-07-12: none have closed and no deadlines have shifted since last check. Belgium's core Sept-2026 finance/accounting/compliance PhD cycle remains closed (confirmed again for KU Leuven corporate finance/governance, actuarial science, insolvency-law FLOF, VUB Auditing, Ghent corporate taxation/finance, HEC Liège, UCLouvain SMIC — all deadline-passed). The 9 open Belgium offers currently live belong to the autumn-2026/2027 continuation cycle (KU Leuven Jan Ronse company law + tax law + marketing CSM/GenAI joint PhD, Ghent EB22 insolvency + accounting-education assistants, VUB Solvay LLM PhD, UHasselt family-business succession, KU Leuven OM/IS agri-tech). Targeted re-searches for brand-new Belgium, Netherlands, and Luxembourg finance/accounting/compliance/banking postings turned up no additional open vacancies beyond the 18 already tracked — AcademicTransfer's newest Dutch finance-adjacent listings (Wageningen phytosanitary economics, additional TU Delft climate-risk projects) are outside the finance/accounting/compliance/banking domain scope or already past deadline. OSINT enrichment pass added: confirmed email + official profile for Dr. ir. Marcela Tuler de Oliveira (TU Delft), confirmed email + LinkedIn for Prof. Werner Haslehner (Luxembourg tax law), LinkedIn + official profile for Dr. Christian Wiehenkamp and Dr. Joana Wensing (Maastricht, no institutional email publicly listed — not fabricated), and cited enrolledStudentsSample cohorts for the KU Leuven Jan Ronse Institute (company-law track) and University of Luxembourg Department of Finance doctoral researchers, sourced from official staff/people directories and individually verifiable LinkedIn/personal academic pages.

phdOffers.institution: Maastricht University — School of Business and Economics
phdOffers.title: PhD Candidate: Sustainable, Long-term Investment Decisions
phdOffers.subject: Empirical and theoretical research on sustainable finance, portfolio construction, and long-term value integration in investment decisions.
phdOffers.fundingDetails: 18-month contract + 2.5-year extension; gross €3,059–€3,881/month (CAO-NU PhD scale); 8% holiday + 8.3% year-end bonus; ABP pension.
phdOffers.notes: Re-verified open 2026-07-12 (deadline 3 days out; EURAXESS mirror job/434535 confirms same deadline). Industry internship at Effctl Capital GmbH (Munich). Wiehenkamp is external/alumnus supervisor — no institutional email publicly listed, LinkedIn + Maastricht profile confirmed instead.

phdOffers.institution: Erasmus University Rotterdam — Rotterdam School of Management (ERIM)
phdOffers.title: Fully funded PhD — Health Technology Assessment in Innovative Neurosurgical Care
phdOffers.subject: Health economics / HTA research on innovative neurosurgical care within ERIM LIS programme.
phdOffers.fundingDetails: Up to 4-year salaried promovendus contract (CAO-NU); ERIM Support Programme for conferences and research costs.
phdOffers.notes: Requires MSc in business/economics or related; GMAT/GRE and IELTS/TOEFL per ERIM norms. Not re-fetched this wave (deadline far out, 08-16); no change from prior verification.

phdOffers.institution: Delft University of Technology — Faculty TPM
phdOffers.title: PhD — Digital Trust (Digitaal Vertrouwen)
phdOffers.subject: Socio-technical architecture for qualified digital identity and verification (eIDAS), including financial services and tax/real-estate use cases.
phdOffers.fundingDetails: 4-year contract (1.5y + 2.5y); gross €3,059–€3,881/month (CAO-NU); partners include Belastingdienst and financial-sector stakeholders.

supervisors.name: Dr. ir. Marcela Tuler de Oliveira

phdOffers.notes: Sum Volo consortium (14 orgs). Dutch fluency required. Co-supervisor email confirmed 2026-07-12 via official TU Delft staff directory (www.tudelft.nl/en/staff/m.tulerdeoliveira).

phdOffers.institution: University of Luxembourg — Department of Finance
phdOffers.title: Doctoral Researcher Positions in Finance
phdOffers.subject: PhD in finance via MQEF first year then thesis research; asset pricing, corporate finance, financial econometrics.
phdOffers.fundingDetails: Yearly gross salary EUR 41,976 (full-time); 36-month contract; MQEF year 1 then doctoral track up to 5 years total.
phdOffers.notes: Dual application: MQEF website + University HR portal (ref UOL07958). Rolling review, open until filled per vacancyedu.com mirror. Enrolled-student sample sourced from official FDEF Department of Finance people page and individually verifiable LinkedIn/personal academic pages (2026-07-12).

phdOffers.institution: University of Luxembourg — Faculty of Law, Economics and Finance
phdOffers.title: Doctoral Researcher in Tax Law
phdOffers.subject: Doctoral thesis in tax law with relevance to Luxembourg financial centre and EU fiscal framework.
phdOffers.fundingDetails: Yearly gross salary EUR 43,445 (full-time); 36-month fixed-term doctoral contract.
phdOffers.notes: Master in tax law required. Job ref UOL08217. Supervisor email + LinkedIn confirmed 2026-07-12 via official FDEF people page (ATOZ Chair for European and International Taxation).

phdOffers.institution: Maastricht University — School of Business and Economics
phdOffers.title: PhD Candidate: AI and Sustainable Consumer Choices
phdOffers.subject: Behavioral economics / consumer finance research on sustainable decision-making and conversational AI.
phdOffers.notes: Behavioral/marketing angle; requires 1-page research proposal. Wensing is Assistant Professor at Maastricht Sustainability Institute (not marketing dept) — official profile + LinkedIn confirmed 2026-07-12, no public institutional email found (not fabricated).

phdOffers.institution: Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam — Faculty of Law (ATLANTIS / ALTI)
phdOffers.title: PhD — Computational Antitrust: Data Access by Competition Agencies (Project A)
phdOffers.subject: Legal framework for EU/national competition agencies' data collection under Reg 1/2003, GDPR, and fundamental rights.
phdOffers.fundingDetails: ERC Consolidator Grant ATLANTIS; 4-year CAO-NU contract (€3,059–€3,881/month); travel budget for agency fieldwork.
phdOffers.notes: Legal-track; competition law expertise required. No change from prior wave.

phdOffers.institution: Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam — Faculty of Law (ATLANTIS / ALTI)
phdOffers.title: PhD — Computational Antitrust: AI Fairness in Agency Enforcement (Project B)
phdOffers.subject: Legal regime for AI use by competition agencies: bias mitigation, transparency, explainability, and procedural fairness.
phdOffers.fundingDetails: ERC ATLANTIS grant; 4-year salaried PhD (CAO-NU); max 20% teaching.
phdOffers.notes: Legal-track; AI Act and administrative enforcement focus. No change from prior wave.

phdOffers.institution: Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam — Faculty of Law (ATLANTIS / ALTI)
phdOffers.title: PhD — Computational Antitrust: Cross-Project Technical Track
phdOffers.subject: Empirical data documentation and AI bias auditing for competition agency computational tools.
phdOffers.fundingDetails: ERC ATLANTIS; 4-year CAO-NU PhD contract with personal travel budget.
phdOffers.notes: Computational-track; CS/AI background preferred. No change from prior wave.

phdOffers.institution: KU Leuven — Jan Ronse Institute for Company and Financial Law
phdOffers.title: PhD in company and/or association law (100%)
phdOffers.subject: Doctoral dissertation in corporate law and/or associations law within the company-law branch of the Jan Ronse Institute; topic defined with supervisor; limited Dutch-language teaching assistance.
phdOffers.fundingDetails: Full-time appointment 1 year, renewable up to 4 years upon positive evaluation; KU Leuven doctoral researcher salary scales.
phdOffers.notes: Master of Law or Master in Economics, Law and Business Administration (cum laude); excellent Dutch required. Re-verified open 2026-07-12 (deadline 2026-07-20). Enrolled-student sample = current company-law-track (COMP) doctoral researchers listed on the official Jan Ronse Institute staff page.

phdOffers.institution: KU Leuven — Institute for Tax Law (Economic Law)
phdOffers.title: PhD position in tax law (100%)
phdOffers.subject: Fundamental doctoral research in tax law; candidates submit a concrete research proposal; limited teaching tasks (exercises, thesis reading, exam supervision).
phdOffers.fundingDetails: Full-time fixed-term 1 year, renewable up to 4 years; KU Leuven doctoral contract.
phdOffers.notes: Master with strong tax-law focus; research proposal required. Re-verified open 2026-07-12 (deadline 2026-07-31), unchanged.

phdOffers.institution: Ghent University — Department of Accounting, Corporate Finance and Taxation (EB22)
phdOffers.title: Assistant — doctoral research in insolvency law (ref. 202605/EB22/AS/011)
phdOffers.subject: PhD dissertation at the intersection of law and business economics, focusing on insolvency law; ≥50% research; assist teaching introductory/private/economic law courses.
phdOffers.fundingDetails: Two-year AAP appointment (salary scale AAP3), renewable twice for two years (max 6 years per Flemish HE rules); doctoral track.
phdOffers.notes: Master in Business Administration or Law; Dutch teaching required. Re-verified open 2026-07-12 (deadline 2026-07-28), unchanged.

phdOffers.institution: Ghent University — Department of Accounting, Corporate Finance and Taxation (EB22)
phdOffers.title: Assistant — doctoral research in accounting (ref. 202607/EB22/AS/018)
phdOffers.subject: PhD in accounting with potential focus on accounting education; ≥50% research; teach Dutch financial accounting tutorials (Accounting A/B, team-based learning).
phdOffers.fundingDetails: Two-year AAP3 appointment, renewable twice (max 6 years); UGent staff benefits.
phdOffers.notes: Apply via UGent e-recruitment linked from Academic Positions listing. Re-verified open 2026-07-12 (deadline 2026-07-28), unchanged; ref. 202607/EB22/AS/018 confirmed distinct from insolvency-law ref. 202605/EB22/AS/011.

phdOffers.institution: Vrije Universiteit Brussel — Solvay Business School (Data Analytics Lab)
phdOffers.title: PhD student in Large Language Models, Innovation, and Science
phdOffers.subject: Research on LLMs, automated science, and innovation — measuring LLM capabilities on economically relevant tasks, detecting model gaming, and methodological differences vs human scientists.
phdOffers.fundingDetails: Full-time PhD scholarship 12 months (extendable to max. 48 months); government salary scale; VUB benefits.
phdOffers.notes: Commercial Engineering or Applied Economics accepted; Dutch + English required. Re-confirmed still listed live on jobs.vub.be PhD board 2026-07-12 (VUB Auditing PhD from same faculty confirmed closed same date, for contrast).

phdOffers.institution: KU Leuven — Faculty of Economics and Business (Marketing)
phdOffers.title: PhD position in Marketing Strategy — Customer Success Management & firm performance
phdOffers.subject: Empirical research on whether Customer Success Management (CSM) creates organizational and financial value — investor reactions, long-term firm performance, subscription-economy contexts.
phdOffers.fundingDetails: Full-time funded PhD 4 years (yearly evaluation); KU Leuven doctoral salary; conference and training support.
phdOffers.notes: Quantitative MSc (business engineering, econometrics, data science); event-study / DiD skills valued. Re-verified open 2026-07-12; supervisor LinkedIn confirmed via his own recruiting post for these two positions.

phdOffers.institution: KU Leuven — Faculty of Economics and Business (Marketing) & University of Melbourne
phdOffers.title: Joint PhD — GenAI implementation in customer service (KU Leuven & Melbourne)
phdOffers.subject: Internationally funded project on GenAI in customer service: firm value effects, employee role changes, reskilling programs — event studies, field studies, advanced econometrics.
phdOffers.fundingDetails: Full-time funded PhD 4 years; minimum 1 year research stay at University of Melbourne; dual degree on completion.
phdOffers.notes: Melbourne co-supervision; 1–2 years abroad required. Re-verified open 2026-07-12; all three supervisor LinkedIn URLs confirmed via Van Vaerenbergh's own recruiting post.

phdOffers.institution: Hasselt University — Research Center for Entrepreneurship and Family Firms (RCEF)
phdOffers.title: Doctoral scholarship — Family business succession: tradition vs innovation
phdOffers.subject: Post-succession dynamics in family firms — balancing tradition and successor-led change; mixed econometric and qualitative case-study methods; teaching in Accountancy, Finance and Governance.
phdOffers.fundingDetails: Full-time doctoral scholarship 2×2 years after positive mid-term evaluation; UHasselt doctoral school membership.
phdOffers.notes: Apply online via UHasselt vacancy portal (linked from listing). Re-verified open 2026-07-12 (deadline 2026-08-14), unchanged. Added a current RCEF doctoral-fellow (Accountancy, Financiering en Governance research group) as cohort context — same research group, not this specific vacancy's incumbent.

phdOffers.institution: KU Leuven — Faculty of Economics and Business (Operations Management & IS)
phdOffers.title: PhD student — Operations Management / Information Systems (soft-fruit agri-tech)
phdOffers.subject: Data-driven and mechanistic modelling for microclimate and pest/disease prediction in protected soft-fruit production; decision-support for growers — OM/IS doctoral programme.
phdOffers.fundingDetails: Full-time PhD up to 4 years (yearly evaluation); salary scale 43; co-supervision with pcfruit vzw.
phdOffers.notes: OM/IS focus; included for quantitative FEB pipeline — weaker direct finance/accounting fit. No change from prior wave; retained at Medium confidence given domain-fit caveat.
